steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence joins two independent clauses with the transition word however. The SAT standard for this construction is: independent clause + semicolon + however + comma + independent clause (IC; however, IC).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'period before however': creates two separate sentences, but the sentence is written as one continuous unit. The period here breaks the sentence in a way that does not match the construction. ✗
  • Choice B — 'semicolon before however, comma after': correctly applies IC; however, IC — the semicolon joins the independent clauses and the comma punctuates the conjunctive adverb. ✓
  • Choice C — 'colon before however': a colon before a transition word is non-standard and does not follow SAT punctuation rules. ✗
  • Choice D — 'comma only': creates a comma splice, which is not acceptable for joining two independent clauses. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
